title: Admin Dashboard: Student Registry, Edit Details, Per-Student Sessions & Materials

requirements.text: Update the admin Students page (frontend/src/pages/admin/Students.tsx) to display all registered students fetched from the backend, showing each student's full details including name, email, contact info, enrolled course, and enrollment/payment status.

quotes: in admin dashboard, I'm not able to see any student who is registered, uh, for which course, for which details
View registered students — see all students, which courses they're enrolled in, and their full details

acceptanceCriteria: Admin Students page fetches and displays all students from the backend via React Query
Each student row/card shows name, email, contact info, enrolled course name, and enrollment/payment status
Page renders an empty state message when no students are registered
Search and filter controls work against the displayed student list

requirements.text: Add an 'Edit Student' capability in the admin Students page that allows admins to edit all fields for a student: name, email, contact info, enrollment status, and any other stored student fields. Changes must be persisted to the backend.

quotes: Editing student details means admin should edit anything, name, email, contact, info, enrollment status, everything.

acceptanceCriteria: An 'Edit' action is available per student in the admin Students page
Clicking Edit opens a modal or inline form pre-populated with the student's current data
Form includes fields for name, email, contact info, enrollment status, and all other student fields stored in the backend
Saving the form calls the backend to persist the changes
The student list refreshes to reflect the updated data after a successful save
Validation prevents saving with empty required fields

requirements.text: Update the admin ManageStudentSessions page (frontend/src/pages/admin/ManageStudentSessions.tsx) to allow admins to select any registered student from a dropdown and add sessions (date, time, Google Meet link, topic) that are stored and displayed exclusively on that specific student's dashboard. Existing backend session hooks (useSessions.ts) must be used.

quotes: the sessions are specific for the students. Means if I add, if I have option in admin portal to add sessions, there is an option to select for that particular student. So it will appear on the student on that, on the particular student dashboard only

acceptanceCriteria: Admin can select any registered student from a dropdown populated from the backend
Session form includes fields for date, time, Google Meet link, and topic
On submission, the session is saved and associated only with the selected student
The session appears in the selected student's sessions list on their student dashboard
The session does NOT appear on other students' dashboards
Admin can view and delete sessions per selected student
Sessions list updates immediately after adding or deleting

requirements.text: Update the admin ManageStudentMaterials page (frontend/src/pages/admin/ManageStudentMaterials.tsx) to allow admins to select any registered student from a dropdown and add study materials (title, description, link/URL) that are stored and displayed exclusively on that specific student's dashboard. Existing backend materials hooks (useMaterials.ts) must be used.

quotes: similar things for the materials similar for materials same means materials same for materials

acceptanceCriteria: Admin can select any registered student from a dropdown populated from the backend
Materials form includes fields for title, description, and a file/URL link
On submission, the material is saved and associated only with the selected student
The material appears in the selected student's materials page on their student dashboard
The material does NOT appear on other students' dashboards
Admin can view and delete materials per selected student
Materials list updates immediately after adding or deleting

requirements.text: Ensure the admin Dashboard overview page (frontend/src/pages/admin/Dashboard.tsx) correctly fetches and displays the recent students list and summary stats (total students, active, pending, payments) from the backend using existing React Query hooks.

quotes: in admin dashboard, I'm not able to see any student who is registered

acceptanceCriteria: Admin Dashboard stat cards show accurate counts fetched from the backend
Recent students section lists students with their enrolled course and status
Data is sourced from backend via React Query, not localStorage

requirements.text: Extend the backend (backend/main.mo) with an updateStudent function that allows admins to update any field of a registered student record (name, email, contact info, enrollment status, and any other stored fields). Restrict this function to admin-only access.

quotes: Editing student details means admin should edit anything, name, email, contact, info, enrollment status, everything.

acceptanceCriteria: A new updateStudent backend function accepts a student identifier and a record of fields to update
The function updates the student record in stable storage
Only authenticated admins can call this function; non-admins receive an error
The updated student data is immediately returned or queryable via existing getStudents/getStudent calls

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo (single-actor architecture)
Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
Per-student sessions and materials must remain isolated — a session or material added for Student A must never appear on Student B's dashboard
Use existing React Query hooks (useSessions.ts, useMaterials.ts, useQueries.ts) where available; extend them if needed rather than bypassing them
nonGoals: Adding course-level (shared) sessions or materials visible to all students in a course
Changing the student registration or payment flow
Modifying the student-facing portal UI beyond displaying sessions and materials correctly
Adding new admin roles or permission levels beyond the existing admin/student split
